mechanism_narrative: A stable or declining FEDFUNDS rate signals easier discounting conditions and lower equity risk premia, which lifts S&P 500 prices over the next month. The effect is strongest when inflation is contained, because the market treats unchanged policy as accommodation rather than restraint.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher than baseline when FEDFUNDS is flat or falling, or if the signal's Rank IC stays below 0.03 for any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend marks worsening labor demand and higher recession risk, which leads investors to reprice earnings down and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show lower 20-day SPX returns than non-triggered episodes, or if more than 60% of triggered cases have nonnegative 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Improving INDPRO with rising PAYEMS indicates broad real-sector acceleration that supports earnings expectations and cyclicals, translating into positive S&P 500 performance over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly-like monthly momentum in INDPRO and PAYEMS does not correspond to higher 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect turns negative in a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in RSAFS and HOUST signals resilient consumer spending and housing demand, which supports revenue growth expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is not decomposable into either series alone; both must strengthen together to confirm broad demand momentum.
failure_condition: Reject if joint RSAFS-HOUST upswings do not outperform single-series signals, or if the combined trigger yields no excess positive 20-day return frequency versus baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains below the inflation trend, real policy becomes looser only if inflation is still contained; otherwise the market interprets it as future tightening risk and discounts equities. The positive equity effect is therefore conditional on inflation staying near target.
failure_condition: Reject if the trigger does not improve 5-day SPX returns versus unconditional days, or if the sign becomes negative when inflation is below the stated threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A payroll slowdown in PAYEMS combined with rising UNRATE identifies an inflection from expansion to contraction in labor income, which typically compresses earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth turns negative over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if payroll-negative episodes with rising unemployment fail to underperform, or if 60-day forward returns are positive in a majority of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A firm advance in GDPC1 indicates stronger aggregate final demand and better earnings visibility, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is strongest when the GDP release confirms acceleration relative to the prior quarter rather than mere level strength.
failure_condition: Reject if above-trend GDPC1 growth does not correspond to higher 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal shows no positive separation from baseline over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, rising FEDFUNDS combined with weakening INDPRO signals policy is biting into real activity, which raises recession odds and pushes S&P 500 returns negative over the next month. The effect fails when industrial output keeps rising despite higher rates.
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate episodes with falling INDPRO do not produce lower 20-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal is not worse than rate-only signals in out-of-sample tests.
